title: salix aurita var. cinerea
intro: Salix aurita var. cinerea, also known as the gray-puss sallow, is a variety of willow tree characterized by its grayish foliage and distinctive catkin-bearing branches. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which have been traditionally employed to treat wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Salix aurita var. cinerea include reducing fever, alleviating pain and inflammation, and acting as an antimicrobial agent to combat infections. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves that can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
